rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SHARP VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, AUGUST 4, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Foster Wheeler LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s declaration is not competent evidence because it was not executed under penalty of perjury. (CCP 2015.5) = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JEROME SHERFY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, AUGUST 4, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present sufficient evidence creating a triable issue that any of the insulation disturbed by defendant in plaintiff¿s presence contained asbestos. Based on the evidence presented on this motion, because the insulation was from the 1920s or 1930s did not have a green band is an insufficient basis for a reasonable trier of fact to conclude that the insulation contained asbestos. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT TIBURCIO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, AUGUST 4, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is continued on the court¿s own motion to August 10, 2011 to permit plaintiff to file supplemental opposition papers, if he so desires, on the issue of whether the court should consider the excerpts of plaintiff¿s deposition that were not submitted until defendant¿s reply papers, but were referred to in both defendant¿s moving papers and plaintiff¿s opposition papers. Any supplemental opposition papers should be filed and served no later than August 8, 2011. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT TIBURCIO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, AUGUST 4, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Albay Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and off calendar as to strict liability and punitive damages. As to negligence, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories identified David Wilson as a person with knowledge of facts supporting plaintiff¿s contention that he was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s responses further stated that Mr. Wilson was a Brayton Purcell client, and included a summary of the knowledge Mr. Wilson possessed. Defendant¿s separate statement failed to demonstrate that Mr. Wilson does not have such knowledge. Defendant is thus precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to strict liability and punitive damages, plaintiff represents that he is dismissing those claims. Moreover, the declarations of Mr. Wilson and Mr. Ay create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos disturbed by defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CASEY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike Or Tax Defendant Perini Corporation¿S Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, AUGUST 4, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Plaintiffs¿ motion to strike or tax defendant Perini Corp.¿s memorandum of costs is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)
